children.heading: .—Surviving spouse or former spouse
children.label: 1 .—Surviving spouse or former spouse
children.text: The eligible surviving spouse or the eligible former spouse.

children.heading: .—Surviving children
children.label: 2 .—Surviving children
children.text: The surviving dependent children in equal shares, if the eligible surviving spouse or the eligible former spouse is dead, dies, or otherwise becomes ineligible under this section.

children.heading: .—Dependent children
children.label: 3 .—Dependent children
children.text: The dependent children in equal shares if the person to whom applies (with the concurrence of the person’s spouse, if required under ) elected to provide an annuity for dependent children but not for the spouse or former spouse.section 1448 of this titlesection 1448(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: .—Special needs trusts for sole benefit of certain dependent children
children.label: 4 .—Special needs trusts for sole benefit of certain dependent children
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (i), a supplemental or special needs trust established under subparagraph (A) or (C) of section 1917(d)(4) of the Social Security Act () for the sole benefit of a dependent child considered disabled under section 1614(a)(3) of that Act () who is incapable of self-support because of mental or physical incapacity.42 U.S.C. 1396p(d)(4)42 U.S.C. 1382c(a)(3)

children.heading: .—Natural person designated under “insurable interest” coverage
children.label: 5 .—Natural person designated under “insurable interest” coverage
children.text: The natural person designated under , unless the election to provide an annuity to the natural person has been changed as provided in subsection (f).section 1448(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: 55,Termination of Annuity for Death, Remarriage Before AgeEtc.—
children.label: b 55,Termination of Annuity for Death, Remarriage Before AgeEtc.—

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: 1 .—General rule
children.text: An annuity payable to the beneficiary terminates effective as of the first day of the month in which eligibility is lost.

children.heading: .—Termination of spouse annuity upon death or remarriage before age 55
children.label: 2 .—Termination of spouse annuity upon death or remarriage before age 55
children.text: An annuity for a surviving spouse or former spouse shall be paid to the surviving spouse or former spouse while the surviving spouse or former spouse is living or, if the surviving spouse or former spouse remarries before reaching age 55, until the surviving spouse or former spouse remarries.

children.heading: .—Effect of termination of subsequent marriage before age 55
children.label: 3 .—Effect of termination of subsequent marriage before age 55
children.text: If the surviving spouse or former spouse remarries before reaching age 55 and that marriage is terminated by death, annulment, or divorce, payment of the annuity shall be resumed effective as of the first day of the month in which the marriage is so terminated. However, if the surviving spouse or former spouse is also entitled to an annuity under the Plan based upon the marriage so terminated, the surviving spouse or former spouse may not receive both annuities but must elect which to receive.

children.heading: Offset for Amount of Dependency and Indemnity Compensation.—
children.label: c Offset for Amount of Dependency and Indemnity Compensation.—
A
children.text: During the period beginning on , and ending on , the amount that the annuity otherwise payable under this section would exceed such dependency and indemnity compensation.January 1, 2020December 31, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: During the period beginning on , and ending on , the amount that the annuity otherwise payable under this section would exceed two-thirds of such dependency and indemnity compensation.January 1, 2021December 31, 2021

children.label: C
children.text: During the period beginning on , and ending on , the amount that the annuity otherwise payable under this section would exceed one-third of such dependency and indemnity compensation.January 1, 2022December 31, 2022

children.label: D
children.text: On and after , the full amount of the annuity under this section.January 1, 2023

children.heading: .—Effective date of offset
children.label: 2 .—Effective date of offset
children.text: A reduction in an annuity under this section required by paragraph (1) shall be effective on the date of the commencement of the period of payment of such dependency and indemnity compensation under title 38.

children.label: A
children.text: a single notice of the net amount to be recouped or the net amount to be refunded, as applicable, under this subsection or subsection (e);

children.label: B
children.text: a written explanation of the statutory requirements for recoupment of the offset amount and for refund of any applicable amount deducted from retired pay;

children.label: C
children.text: a detailed accounting of how the offset amount being recouped and retired pay deduction amount being refunded were calculated; and

children.label: D
children.text: contact information for a person who can provide information about the offset recoupment and retired pay deduction refund processes and answer questions the surviving spouse or former spouse may have about the requirements, processes, or amounts.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Payment of Annuities When Coverage Under Civil Service Retirement Elected
children.label: d .—Limitation on Payment of Annuities When Coverage Under Civil Service Retirement Elected
children.text: If, upon the death of a person to whom applies, that person had in effect a waiver of that person’s retired pay for the purposes of subchapter III of chapter 83 of title 5 or chapter 84 of such title, an annuity under this section shall not be payable unless, in accordance with section 8339(j) or 8416(a) of title 5, that person notified the Office of Personnel Management that he did not desire any spouse surviving him to receive an annuity under section 8341(b) or 8442(a) of that title.section 1448 of this title

children.heading: Refund of Amounts Deducted From Retired Pay or CRSC When DIC Offset Is Applicable.—
children.label: e Refund of Amounts Deducted From Retired Pay or CRSC When DIC Offset Is Applicable.—

children.heading: .—Full refund when dic greater than sbp annuity
children.label: 1 .—Full refund when dic greater than sbp annuity
children.text: If an annuity under this section is not payable because of subsection (c), any amount deducted from the retired pay or combat-related special compensation of the deceased under shall be refunded to the surviving spouse or former spouse.section 1452 of this title

children.heading: .—Partial refund when sbp annuity reduced by dic
children.label: 2 .—Partial refund when sbp annuity reduced by dic
children.text: If, because of subsection (c), the annuity payable is less than the amount established under , the annuity payable shall be recalculated under that section. The amount of the reduction in the retired pay required to provide that recalculated annuity shall be computed under , and the difference between the amount deducted before the computation of that recalculated annuity and the amount that would have been deducted on the basis of that recalculated annuity shall be refunded to the surviving spouse or former spouse.section 1451 of this titlesection 1452 of this title

children.heading: Change in Election of Insurable Interest or Former Spouse Beneficiary.—
children.label: f Change in Election of Insurable Interest or Former Spouse Beneficiary.—

children.heading: .—Election in favor of spouse or child
children.label: A .—Election in favor of spouse or child
children.text: A person who elects to provide an annuity to a person designated by him under may, subject to paragraph (2), change that election and provide an annuity to his spouse or dependent child.section 1448(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: B .—Notice
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall notify the former spouse or other natural person previously designated under of any change of election under subparagraph (A).section 1448(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Procedures, effective date, etc
children.label: C .—Procedures, effective date, etc
children.text: Any such change of election is subject to the same rules with respect to execution, revocation, and effectiveness as are set forth in (without regard to the eligibility of the person making the change of election to make such an election under that section). Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, a change of election under this subsection to provide an annuity to a spouse instead of a former spouse may (subject to paragraph (2)) be made at any time after the person providing the annuity remarries without regard to the time limitation in .section 1448(a)(5) of this titlesection 1448(a)(5)(B) of this title

children.heading: .—Limitation on change in beneficiary when former spouse coverage in effect
children.label: 2 .—Limitation on change in beneficiary when former spouse coverage in effect
i
children.text: furnishes to the Secretary concerned a certified copy of a court order which is regular on its face and which modifies the provisions of all previous court orders relating to such election, or the agreement to make such election, so as to permit the person to change the election; and

children.label: ii
children.text: certifies to the Secretary concerned that the court order is valid and in effect.

children.label: i
children.text: furnishes to the Secretary concerned a statement, in such form as the Secretary concerned may prescribe, signed by the former spouse and evidencing the former spouse’s agreement to a change in the election under paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: certifies to the Secretary concerned that the statement is current and in effect.

children.heading: Required former spouse election to be deemed to have been made.—
children.label: 3 Required former spouse election to be deemed to have been made.—

children.heading: .—Deemed election upon request by former spouse
children.label: A .—Deemed election upon request by former spouse

children.heading: .—Request from former spouse
children.label: i .—Request from former spouse
children.text: A written request, in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe, from the former spouse concerned requesting that such an election be deemed to have been made.

children.heading: .—Copy of court order or other official statement
children.label: ii .—Copy of court order or other official statement
I
children.text: a copy of the court order, regular on its face, which requires such election or incorporates, ratifies, or approves the written agreement of such person; or

children.label: II
children.text: a statement from the clerk of the court (or other appropriate official) that such agreement has been filed with the court in accordance with applicable State law.

children.label: i
children.text: the person enters, incident to a proceeding of divorce, dissolution, or annulment, into a written agreement to make such an election and the agreement (I) has been incorporated in or ratified or approved by a court order, or (II) has been filed with the court of appropriate jurisdiction in accordance with applicable State law; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the person is required by a court order to make such an election.

children.heading: .—Time limit for request by former spouse
children.label: C .—Time limit for request by former spouse
children.text: An election may not be deemed to have been made under subparagraph (A) in the case of any person unless the Secretary concerned receives a request from the former spouse of the person within one year of the date of the court order or filing involved.

children.heading: .—Effective date of deemed election
children.label: D .—Effective date of deemed election
children.text: An election deemed to have been made under subparagraph (A) shall become effective on the day referred to in .section 1448(b)(3)(E)(ii) of this title

children.heading: .—Former spouse coverage may be required by court order
children.label: 4 .—Former spouse coverage may be required by court order
children.text: A court order may require a person to elect (or to enter into an agreement to elect) under to provide an annuity to a former spouse (or to both a former spouse and child).section 1448(b) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on Changing or Revoking Elections.—
children.label: g Limitation on Changing or Revoking Elections.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An election under this section may not be changed or revoked.

children.label: A
children.text: a revocation of an election under ; orsection 1449(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a change in an election under subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Treatment of Annuities Under Other Laws
children.label: h .—Treatment of Annuities Under Other Laws
children.text: Except as provided in , an annuity under this section is in addition to any other payment to which a person is entitled under any other provision of law. Such annuity shall be considered as income under laws administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.section 1451 of this title

children.heading: .—Annuities Exempt From Certain Legal Process
children.label: i .—Annuities Exempt From Certain Legal Process
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (a)(4) or ()(3)(B), an annuity under this section is not assignable or subject to execution, levy, attachment, garnishment, or other legal process.l

children.heading: Effective Date of Reserve-Component Annuities.—
children.label: j Effective Date of Reserve-Component Annuities.—

children.heading: (e) .—Persons making section 1448designation
children.label: 1 (e) .—Persons making section 1448designation
children.text: A reserve-component annuity shall be effective in accordance with the designation made under by the person providing the annuity.section 1448(e) of this title

children.heading: (e) .—Persons dying before making section 1448designation
children.label: 2 (e) .—Persons dying before making section 1448designation
children.text: An annuity payable under shall be effective on the day after the date of the death of the person upon whose service the right to the annuity is based.section 1448(f) of this title

children.heading: Adjustment of Spouse or Former Spouse Annuity Upon Loss of Dependency and Indemnity Compensation.—
children.label: k Adjustment of Spouse or Former Spouse Annuity Upon Loss of Dependency and Indemnity Compensation.—

children.heading: .—Readjustment if beneficiary 55 years of age or more
children.label: 1 .—Readjustment if beneficiary 55 years of age or more
children.text: If a surviving spouse or former spouse whose annuity has been adjusted under subsection (c) subsequently loses entitlement to dependency and indemnity compensation under because of the remarriage of the surviving spouse, or former spouse, and if at the time of such remarriage the surviving spouse or former spouse is 55 years of age or more, the amount of the annuity of the surviving spouse or former spouse shall be readjusted, effective on the effective date of such loss of dependency and indemnity compensation, to the amount of the annuity which would be in effect with respect to the surviving spouse or former spouse if the adjustment under subsection (c) had never been made.section 1311(a) of title 38

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: A .—General rule
children.text: A surviving spouse or former spouse whose annuity is readjusted under paragraph (1) shall repay any amount refunded under subsection (e) by reason of the adjustment under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Interest required if repayment not a lump sum
children.label: B .—Interest required if repayment not a lump sum
children.text: If the repayment is not made in a lump sum, the surviving spouse or former spouse shall pay interest on the amount to be repaid. Such interest shall commence on the date on which the first such payment is due and shall be applied over the period during which any part of the repayment remains to be paid.

children.heading: .—Manner of repayment; rate of interest
children.label: C .—Manner of repayment; rate of interest
children.text: The manner in which such repayment shall be made, and the rate of any such interest, shall be prescribed in regulations under .section 1455 of this title

children.heading: .—Deposit of amounts repaid
children.label: D .—Deposit of amounts repaid
children.text: An amount repaid under this paragraph (including any such interest) received by the Secretary of Defense shall be deposited into the Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund. Any other amount repaid under this paragraph shall be deposited into the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: Participants in the Plan Who Are Missing.—
children.label: l Participants in the Plan Who Are Missing.—

children.heading: Authority to presume death of missing participant.—
children.label: 1 Authority to presume death of missing participant.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Upon application of the beneficiary of a participant in the Plan who is missing, the Secretary concerned may determine for purposes of this subchapter that the participant is presumed dead.

children.label: i
children.text: the retired pay of the participant has been suspended on the basis that the participant is missing; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a participant in the Plan who would be eligible for reserve-component retired pay but for the fact that he is under 60 years of age, his retired pay, if he were entitled to retired pay, would be suspended on the basis that he is missing.

children.heading: .—Requirements applicable to presumption of death
children.label: C .—Requirements applicable to presumption of death
i
children.text: the participant has been missing for at least 30 days; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the circumstances under which the participant is missing would lead a reasonably prudent person to conclude that the participant is dead.

children.heading: .—Commencement of annuity
children.label: 2 .—Commencement of annuity
children.text: Upon a determination under paragraph (1) with respect to a participant in the Plan, an annuity otherwise payable under this subchapter shall be paid as if the participant died on the date as of which the retired pay of the participant was suspended.

children.heading: Effect of person not being dead.—
children.label: 3 Effect of person not being dead.—
i
children.text: any annuity being paid under this subchapter by reason of this subsection shall be terminated; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total amount of any annuity payments made by reason of this subsection shall constitute a debt to the United States.

children.heading: .—Collection from participant of annuity amounts erroneously paid
children.label: B .—Collection from participant of annuity amounts erroneously paid
i
children.text: from any retired pay otherwise payable to the participant;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the participant is entitled to compensation under chapter 11 of title 38, from that compensation; or

children.label: iii
children.text: if the participant is entitled to any other payment from the United States, from that payment.

children.heading: .—Collection from beneficiary
children.label: C .—Collection from beneficiary
children.text: If the participant dies before the full recovery of the amount of annuity payments described in subparagraph (A)(ii) has been made by the United States, the remaining amount of such annuity payments may be collected from the participant’s beneficiary under the Plan if that beneficiary was the recipient of the annuity payments made by reason of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the surviving spouse or former spouse is entitled to dependency and indemnity compensation under ;section 1311(a) of title 38

children.label: B
children.text: except for subsection (c) of this section, the surviving spouse or former spouse is eligible for an annuity by reason of a participant in the Plan under subsection (a)(1) of or by reason of coverage under subsection (d) or (f) of such section; andsection 1448 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the eligibility of the surviving spouse or former spouse for an annuity as described in subparagraph (B) is affected by subsection (c) of this section.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: The amount of the allowance paid to an eligible survivor under paragraph (1) for any month may not exceed the amount of the annuity for that month that is subject to offset under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Status of payments
children.label: 4 .—Status of payments
children.text: An allowance paid under this subsection does not constitute an annuity, and amounts so paid are not subject to adjustment under any other provision of law.

children.heading: .—Source of funds
children.label: 5 .—Source of funds
children.text: The special survivor indemnity allowance shall be paid from amounts in the Department of Defense Military Retirement Fund established under .section 1461 of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Whenever retired pay is increased for a month under (or any other provision of law), the amount of the allowance payable under paragraph (1) for that month shall also be increased.section 1401a of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the amount payable pursuant to paragraph (2) for months during the preceding 12-month period; plus

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount equal to a percentage of the amount determined pursuant to clause (i), which percentage is the percentage by which the retired pay of the member would have increased for the month, as described in subparagraph (A), if the member was alive (and otherwise entitled to such pay).

children.heading: .—Rounding down
children.label: C .—Rounding down
children.text: The monthly amount of an allowance payable under this subsection, if not a multiple of $1, shall be rounded to the next lower multiple of $1.

children.heading: .—Public notice on amount of allowance payable
children.label: D .—Public notice on amount of allowance payable
children.text: Whenever an increase in the amount of the allowance payable under paragraph (1) is made pursuant to this paragraph, the Secretary of Defense shall publish the amount of the allowance so payable by reason of such increase, including the months for which payable.
